import pytest
from source import bitcount

def test_bitcount_fails_on_buggy_code():
    # Input with multiple 1-bits: 127 in binary is 0b1111111
    n = 127
    expected_count = 7  # 127 has seven 1-bits

    # The test should fail on the buggy code, as the loop will not terminate
    with pytest.raises(RecursionError, match="maximum recursion depth exceeded"):
        bitcount(n)
    
    # Confirm that with the fixed code, it passes
    assert bitcount(n) == expected_count, f"Expected {expected_count} 1-bits in {n}, got {bitcount(n)}"

def test_bitcount_terminates_correctly():
    # Input with multiple 1-bits: 127 (binary 1111111)
    n = 127
    expected_bit_count = 7  # Number of 1-bits in binary representation of 127

    result = bitcount(n)
    assert result == expected_bit_count, f"Expected bit count of {expected_bit_count} for input {n}, but got {result}"

import pytest

def test_bitcount_fails_on_buggy_code():

    n = 127
    expected_count = 7
